hero.headline: Cash needs a job.
hero.subheadline:
Payroll and rent can't wait. The rest doesn't have to sit still.

diBoaS gives your business the opportunity to put idle cash to work, earning every day, on your terms, under your control.

cardA.headline: Card fees add up.
cardA.body:
Every swipe, tap, and online checkout has a cost.

For many small businesses, card processing fees can take 1.5% to 3.5% of a transaction before the money reaches the account.

diBoaS is designed to help businesses review digital-dollar payment paths where available, with costs shown before funds move.

Not every rail disappears. Not every cost goes away. The point is clarity before approval.

cardB.headline: Idle cash still has a job.
cardB.body:
Every business needs cash ready.

But not every dollar needs to sit in the same account doing the same job.

Set an operating floor. Keep what you need available. Review selected digital-dollar strategies for excess cash only when the risk fits your policy.

Idle cash should not move because a landing page got excited. It should move because the rule is clear.

paragraphs.0: A customer pays today. The money lands later.
paragraphs.1: A card fee takes its cut.
paragraphs.2: Payroll is due Friday. Rent does not wait. Taxes do not care about your cash flow.
paragraphs.3: And the money you keep aside? It often sits in one account, with very little explanation of what else may be possible.
paragraphs.4: diBoaS exists to make business cash easier to separate, move, and understand. Not with promises. With rules, visible costs, visible risk, and wallet control.

connect.title: Connect your flow
connect.description: Link supported business accounts, wallets, or payment paths where available.

rules.title: Set your floor
rules.description: Decide what must stay ready for payroll, rent, taxes, suppliers, and emergencies.

works.title: Review the path
works.description: See strategy, cost, liquidity, and risk before funds move.

access.title: Move when approved
access.description: Your business approves movement from its wallet. Availability may depend on partners, networks, ramps, liquidity, and compliance review.

howItWorks.transitionHook: That's the flow. Here's what your cash gets.

getPaid.headline: Payment paths with visible costs
getPaid.body: Review digital-dollar payment options where available, with expected costs shown before funds move.

payAnyone.headline: Vendor and contractor payments
payAnyone.body: Send payments through supported rails where available. Network, provider, ramp, settlement, and compliance conditions may apply.

adelaide.headline: Rules for idle cash
adelaide.body: Set an operating floor, separate excess cash, and review selected strategies only when they fit your business risk policy.

records.headline: Records for real operations
records.body: Transaction history, statements, and exportable records designed to support accounting, tax, audit, and internal review.
records.imageAlt: A desk with laptops and printed financial records under review.

features.transitionHook: But here's the part nobody else is doing.

fees.header: No surprise in the fee stack.
fees.subtitle: Clear costs before business funds move.

receive.diboas: $0 only when another diBoaS user pays you in digital dollars; a card-funded payment is an on-ramp (0.48% plus the card processor's cost)
receive.difference: Card and ramp costs apply when a customer pays by card; only digital-dollar transfers between diBoaS users are free
receive.example: A diBoaS user pays you $1,000 in digital dollars: diBoaS fee is $0. A card-funded $1,000 payment runs as an on-ramp: 0.48% ($4.80) plus the card processor's cost.

send.diboas: $0 only between diBoaS users in digital dollars; paying an external account is an off-ramp (0.48%)
send.competitors: Wires and transfers may charge fixed fees
send.difference: Only digital-dollar transfers between diBoaS users are free; paying a non-diBoaS contractor runs as an off-ramp
send.example: Pay a contractor who uses diBoaS $500 in digital dollars: diBoaS fee is $0. Pay an external contractor $500: off-ramp 0.48% ($2.40) applies.

invest.difference: Strategy costs and risks shown before approval
invest.example: Start with $10,000: diBoaS charges $0 to start

sell.example: Sell $10,000: diBoaS fee is $25 (0.39% is $39, capped at $25)

swap.competitors: Spreads or provider costs may apply
swap.difference: diBoaS fee shown before you swap

cashOut.competitors: Fees, spreads, and delays may vary

fees.disclaimer: *diBoaS fees shown. Third-party provider, ramp, network, banking, settlement, compliance, or exchange fees may apply and are shown before confirmation where available.
fees.pricingNote: Pricing comparisons based on publicly available rates as of February 2026. Ranges reflect common pricing across major providers. Actual competitor pricing varies by provider, volume, and agreement terms.
fees.example: A diBoaS user pays you $1,000 in digital dollars: you keep $1,000 in your diBoaS account. Cash out to your bank as an off-ramp: {cashOutRate} applies. A card-funded payment is an on-ramp, with card and ramp costs shown before funds move.
fees.expandableToggle: No hidden fees. No minimum balances. No monthly charges. See all fees.
fees.footerLine: Every cost should be visible before business funds move.
fees.transitionHook: Costs first. Then let's see if this is a fit.

goodFit.title: Good Fit
goodFit.description: Your business wants clearer payment paths, better visibility over idle cash, and rules for what can move, what must stay ready, and what risk is acceptable.
items: You want clearer payment paths, with costs shown before funds move.
You want better visibility over idle cash.
You want rules for what can move and what must stay ready.
You're comfortable defining what risk is acceptable.

notAFit.title: Not a Fit
notAFit.description: You need government deposit insurance above everything else. You have zero tolerance for digital-asset, protocol, liquidity, ramp, network, or provider risk. You need a traditional bank account, not a non-custodial cash layer.
items: You need government deposit insurance above everything else.
You have zero tolerance for digital-asset or protocol risk.
You can't accept liquidity, ramp, network, or provider risk.
You need a traditional bank account, not a non-custodial cash layer.

founder.header: Adelaide saved. The system never explained.

versionA: I grew up watching my grandmother Adelaide navigate a financial system that wasn't designed for her. She deserved better tools. So does every business owner like you.

diBoaS is headquartered in Berlin, Germany, building for businesses in the US, EU, and Brazil. I've spent 20+ years working in Products and IT across Brazil, the US, Japan, and Germany. Now I'm building the financial tool I wish every small business and startup had.
versionB: diBoaS started with my grandmother, Adelaide. She saved with discipline. The system gave her low returns, little explanation, and almost no access to better options.

The same pattern shows up in business. Owners lose money to payment fees. Startups hold runway in accounts that explain very little. After 20 years building products across Brazil, the US, Japan, and Germany, I wanted to build a clearer cash layer: one where money has rules, risk has a name, and control stays with the people responsible for the business.
versionC: I grew up watching my grandmother Adelaide navigate a financial system that wasn't designed for her. She deserved better tools. So does every business owner like you.

We're building diBoaS from Berlin, Germany, for businesses in the US, EU, and Brazil. I've spent 20+ years working in Products and IT across Brazil, the US, Japan, and Germany. Now I'm building the financial tool I wish every small business and startup had.

waitlist.description: Payment costs. Idle balances. Operating floors. Strategy risk. Join the business waitlist and be among the first to test diBoaS for Business when it opens.
waitlist.noSpam: No spam. Just product updates, early access, and what changes before launch.

footer.riskDisclaimer: diBoaS connects you to decentralized finance systems. Returns are not guaranteed. Past performance does not predict future results. Involves risks including code vulnerabilities, market fluctuations, and access challenges. Only use funds you can afford to risk. diBoaS is not a bank. No deposit insurance applies.
footer.micaArticle68: The value of crypto-assets may fluctuate. You may lose some or all of your money. Crypto-assets are not covered by deposit guarantee schemes.
footer.micaArticle7: This crypto-asset marketing communication has not been reviewed or approved by any competent authority in any Member State of the European Union. The offeror of the crypto-asset is solely responsible for the content of this crypto-asset marketing communication.
footer.aiDisclosure: Certain content on this platform, including market analysis and educational materials, is generated or assisted by artificial intelligence. AI-generated content may contain errors or limitations. Users should verify information independently before making financial decisions.
footer.usDisclosure: US Disclosure: diBoaS is a non-custodial interface providing access to decentralized finance protocols. diBoaS is not registered with the SEC, CFTC, FinCEN, or any state regulatory agency. US regulatory treatment of DeFi is evolving. You are responsible for determining whether your use of this interface complies with applicable laws.
footer.fictionalResults: Examples on this page are illustrative and do not represent real businesses or actual results. Calculator projections are based on hypothetical scenarios and historical averages. Actual results will vary.
footer.cryptoDisclosure: Crypto-assets are highly volatile. You may lose some or all of your investment. Past performance is not indicative of future results.
footer.closing: diBoaS is a technology platform, not a bank or financial advisor. All financial decisions are your own responsibility.

seo.title: diBoaS for Business | Cash Needs a Job
seo.description: Payroll, rent, taxes, suppliers, runway. diBoaS helps businesses separate cash by purpose, see digital-dollar paths, and understand cost and risk before funds move.
seo.ogDescription: diBoaS helps businesses separate operating cash, payments, and idle balances into clear digital-dollar paths, with cost, liquidity, and risk visible before funds move.
seo.keywords: treasury management, startup finance, returns, operating cash, business payments, B2B fintech

startupTreasury.headline: You didn't raise it to watch it sit still.
startupTreasury.subheadline: Most of your round is runway you won't touch for months. It can wait somewhere that pays you without ever leaving your control.

body.0: A round lands. Payroll takes a slice every month. The rest — most of it — waits.
body.1: It waits in one account, at one bank, earning close to nothing. You already know what that sentence can cost.
body.2: diBoaS doesn't ask you to gamble your runway. Set the floor for what you need. What sits above it goes to work — and stays yours, earning every day, ready to pull back the moment you need it.

startupTreasury.bridgeLine: Don't take the example's word for it. Run it on your own runway.

cta.secondary: See what your idle cash could do

startupTreasury.imageAlt: A calm, sunlit workspace by a window in the morning light
startupTreasury.ariaLabel: Startup treasury — put idle runway to work
